results.description: A high-clarity user providing detailed, structured data across all logic model sections.

turnResults.user: We are starting the Green Haven Community Garden in West Philadelphia. Our goal is to serve local residents by reducing food insecurity and providing a green space for community building.
turnResults.reply: This sounds like a wonderful and much-needed project for West Philadelphia. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?

turnResults.user: We have two part-time coordinators, $5,000 in seed grants, donated tools from the city, and a partnership with a local horticulture expert for training.
turnResults.reply: That's a fantastic set of resources to start with. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work, and what activities do you run with them?

turnResults.user: We hold weekly planting workshops, maintain 20 raised beds, and run a bi-weekly harvest distribution stand.
turnResults.reply: Thank you, that gives me a clear picture of the core activities you're running: the planting workshops, the ongoing maintenance of the garden beds, and the distribution of the harvest. Thanks, I captured those activities. How do you ensure quality and fidelity?

turnResults.user: We maintain quality by using organic-only soil standards. For fidelity, we use a weekly checklist to ensure every bed is watered and weeded according to the garden manual.
turnResults.reply: That's excellent. Great, I captured your quality and fidelity standards. What outcomes should we expect next?

turnResults.user: Short term, we want residents to learn basic gardening. Medium term, we expect 200lbs of produce per season. Long term, we hope to see a 10% decrease in reported food insecurity among regular members.
turnResults.reply: Thank you for laying those out so clearly. Great, this is complete. I captured the outcomes and can summarize the full logic model now.

results.description: A user providing partial/vague info requiring agent follow-ups and coaching.

turnResults.user: We work with high schoolers in the city to help them get jobs.
turnResults.reply: That's great, helping high schoolers get jobs is critical work. Thanks, I captured the impact details so far. What specific long-term goal should we capture so the impact statement is complete?

turnResults.user: Our long-term goal is 100% college or trade school enrollment for our seniors.
turnResults.reply: That's a fantastic and ambitious goal. Great, I captured your intended impact. Next, what resources support this work?

turnResults.user: We have mentors and some funding from a local bank.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for sharing that. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work, and what activities do you run with them?

turnResults.user: Oh, we also use a local library for meeting space and a licensed curriculum.
turnResults.reply: That's helpful, thank you. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work, and what activities do you run with them?

turnResults.user: We do mentorship meetings.
turnResults.reply: Got it, thank you. What activities should we capture next? Please describe the key actions and outputs.

turnResults.user: Each student meets with a mentor for 2 hours a week to work on college apps and career goals.
turnResults.reply: Thank you, that's very specific and helpful. Thanks, I captured those activities. How do you ensure quality and fidelity?

turnResults.user: We check in with the mentors often.
turnResults.reply: That's a great practice. How do you ensure quality and fidelity standards in delivery?

turnResults.user: We use a standardized mentor handbook (fidelity) and require all mentors to have background checks and 10 hours of training (quality).
turnResults.reply: That's incredibly helpful. Great, I captured your quality and fidelity standards. What outcomes should we expect next?

turnResults.user: We want them to succeed.
turnResults.reply: Of course, that's the ultimate goal – for every student to succeed. What should short term, medium term, and long term outcomes look like?

turnResults.user: Short term is completing one college app. Medium term is getting an acceptance letter. Long term is that enrollment goal I mentioned.
turnResults.reply: Thank you, that's a very clear and logical progression: students complete an application, then receive an acceptance letter, and finally enroll in college or trade school. Great, this is complete. I captured the outcomes and can summarize the full logic model now.

results.description: All-in-one impact statement is captured and confirmed.

turnResults.user: We serve middle school students in North Philadelphia and our long-term goal is that they read on grade level and transition successfully to high school.
turnResults.reply: Thank you for sharing that. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?

turnResults.user: Yes, that captures it.
turnResults.reply: Great, I'm glad we have that clearly defined. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?

results.description: When user provides a resource list, the patch captures multiple resource buckets.

turnResults.user: We have program staff, volunteers, partner counselors, curriculum materials, laptops, grant funding, and staff training.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for that comprehensive list. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

results.description: Resource response should not be ignored across turns and should keep resources in model state.

turnResults.user: Staff mentors, school partners, donated laptops, grant funding, and evidence-based training.
turnResults.reply: Thank you for sharing that. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

turnResults.user: Anything else you need from me on resources?
turnResults.reply: That's a fair question. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?
